event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: Pinned official black-box evidence exposes only the independently observed record roles, scalar types, byte-order classification, exact typed inputs/outcomes, and private driver/raw hashes.
The sample effect requires its selected lock entry, exact runtime marker, and the accepted connection-setup selection; absent or mismatched input remains inert.
One finite raw timestamp and one float32 sample cross the admitted loopback connection with exact source bits and accepted single-channel homogeneous shape.
Non-finite timestamp, malformed/truncated/oversized record, timeout, cancellation, and immediate listener reuse have typed bounded outcomes.
No chunks, other formats, clock correction, queue, retry/recovery, background runtime, devices, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit evidence, closed activation, integrated one-record runtime, tests, and public routing coherently before validation; keep lifecycle and push commits intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the first one-record timestamped sample runtime and retain clock/queue/authority exclusions.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run owner, runtime-loopback, public-boundary, lock, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Describe the opt-in one-record float32 path without broader transport claims.
Document connection composition, record bounds, exact bit preservation, cancellation, and cleanup.
Separate black-box sample framing evidence from the independent Rust runtime claim.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused evidence and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bind official endpoint and private sample-probe hashes without publishing raw payloads.
Route exact-bit, malformed-record, cancellation, deadline, cleanup, and owner checks.
Review public/private and direct-network routing; no shared mutation expected.
Review data-plane ownership and closed activation; no shared mutation expected.

non_scope: Chunks, multiple samples, multiple channels, channel formats other than float32, regular-rate scheduling, clock acquisition/exchange/correction, dejitter, smoothing, queues, backpressure, retry, recovery, background work, async runtime, or persistent sockets.
Endpoint selection, non-loopback reachability, performance, currentness, ecosystem-wide compatibility, TLS, authentication, or authorization.

Official implementation source, copied or translated source, dependencies, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, inbound-sample commands, or Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Extend the accepted bounded connection setup by one independently evidenced protocol-110 record: exactly one caller-supplied finite raw timestamp and one homogeneous single-channel float32 sample cross a caller-selected loopback connection with exact value/timestamp bits, finite allocation and I/O bounds, cancellation, typed malformed input, and scope-owned cleanup.
outputs: Sanitized black-box sample-record evidence binding private raw bytes by SHA-256.
One selected timestamped-float32-sample runtime feature composed over bounded connection setup.
End-to-end loopback tests for exact timestamp/value bits, malformed or truncated records, cancellation, deadline, bounds, and listener reuse.
